summary: Full-stack AI engineer with hands-on experience building and deploying production LLM systems, voice pipelines, and RAG infrastructure end to end. Proven track record shipping AI-driven products across the full stack using modern web frameworks, cloud infrastructure, and backend systems. Comfortable owning projects from data pipeline to deployed inference, with experience in containerization, CI/CD, and scalable backend architecture.

skills.ml: LLMs (Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o-mini), RAG, LangChain, Deepgram, HuggingFace, DistilBERT, XGBoost, YOLO, vector embeddings (Pinecone, OpenAI), SHAP, Captum
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, Railway, GCP, Supabase (PostgreSQL, Row-Level Security), GitHub Actions CI/CD, Vercel, Brevo SMTP
skills.frontend: Next.js, React, FastAPI, Express, REST APIs

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Containerized a YOLO-based object detection model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable production load without code changes.
Developed the YOLO-based detection model in Python to classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.

Built a data augmentation pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases, improving accuracy from 65% to 85.4% on held-out evaluation data.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Shipped a full-stack AI product end to end: engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6, achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.

Built and deployed production AI infrastructure across Railway (backend), Vercel (frontend), and Supabase (PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security and Magic Link authentication), with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

Designed a multi-turn conversational LLM engine with dynamic context injection: the model receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time, plus an automated post-interview evaluation system scoring candidates across 4 dimensions with advance/hold/reject output.
Built an automated document ingestion pipeline with PDF parsing, Claude Haiku summarization, and MD5 deduplication caching to support scalable, low-latency resume processing at session start.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings, deployed full-stack across Railway (FastAPI backend) and Vercel (Next.js frontend).
Set up a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds the Docker image and deploys to production on every merge to main, enabling reliable and repeatable delivery of AI infrastructure updates.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.
Maintained 35+ pytest unit tests and a 55-question domain evaluation set to ensure consistent model quality across deployments.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.
Deployed the model as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces using Docker and GCP, making production inference available to end users without manual intervention.

Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance, and integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI).
Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains to ensure generalization across real-world text distributions.
